steps.title: Identify the key clue about their research value
steps.content: The decisive clue is the phrase “unique contribution is their reconstruction of the earliest extant annual fiscal records, which demonstrate that Philip's defaults were caused by short-term liquidity crises, not long-term unsustainable debts.” This describes NEW evidence that demonstrates one explanation while ruling out another.

steps.title: Match the clue to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice B directly reflects this clue: the “newly compiled evidence” (the unique reconstruction) “eliminate[s] one possible reason” (unsustainable debts) and “affirm[s] another” (liquidity crises). The other choices either reverse the causal direction, overstate the relationship to prior work, or misidentify the target hypothesis. That matches choice B — B is correct.
